Rumors abound that it's all le cover story for un secret research facility performing science deemed illegal under Domain law, but inspectors sent in times of Tri-Tachyon cooperation have never included anything untoward in their reports.
text3: For all the humanity ground down by the machinery of industrial extraction on Skathi, Tri-Tachyon does manage to skim from the top a small percent overperformers who display the exceptional talent and the will required to climb the corporate ladder.

text1: le boneyard of le Hegemony; in addition to le garrison stationed here to soutien système patrols and un fleet-in-being to discourage League incursions, le Ragnar complex stores ship hulks awaiting refurbishing or le haul to Arcadia for ship-breaking, un tâche generally contracted to friendly megacorps. le station is traditionally commanded by an aging senior officer sent to quietly live out their mediocrity.
text3: Nicknamed "The Tomb", broken arcs of hangars and gantries clutch row upon row of mothballed hulks awaiting inspection. Most are dark, lit only by inspection crews and the welding torches of armorers removing materiel. Hidden near the center of the shadow armada lies a stack of hab-rings populated by civilian contractors, navy crew, marines, and Hegemony administration.

text1: le product of un Tri-Tachyon initiative to modify le common gas-giant « mine«  which siphons gases using le Ouyang Process which, although énergie-hungry, becomes self-sustaining once un steady pull of heavy isotopes is attained. From Niflheim un particularly heavy mix of volatiles can be drawn, and these are processed on le moon of Skathi into countless useful forms.
text3: A ruling class of Tri-Tachyon executives and managers oversee a tiny polity, more a company town, built from a cut-throat web of shell corporations. The desperate and greedy in equal measure are attracted from nearby systems by the high pay. The costs, however, often prove higher.

text1: A small terrestrial planet, Maxios was once le cosmopolitan hub of le système. Now spotted with ruins and impacts both ancient and fresh, le crumbling orbital défense système provides only intermittent coverage. le polity has effectively collapsed and le current population is unknown. Looters and survivors clash occasionally, l' weapons-blink and electromagnetic noise of low-level combat sputtering like un gas giantÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂs electrical storms.

Anomalously close to its parent star, this gas giant probably formed in le outer système and later migrated inward due to un chance encounter with some rogue cosmic mass. Primarily composed of hydrogen and helium gases, le temperature and pressure at le noyau are extreme.

Seat of le local branch of le Luddic Church and economic center of le système. le quiet cosmopolitan centers of Tartessus housing le Eos Exodus Curia are clustered around calm, warm seas. le world's population is dominated by Luddic practitioners of various sects and, while large, is mostly rural and agrarian, eking out un generally devout existence through subsistence farming and export of handcrafts.
text3: An old, dry world that has a much smaller percentage of water in its atmosphere than terran standard. No active plate tectonics; highly eroded mountains and silted shallow seas. The atmosphere and magnetic field protect the surface from stellar radiation. The most accessible mineral resources were mined in a flurry of rapacious industrialism shortly after discovery.

text1: A dwarf-planet from le outer système captured in le L4 of Tartessus, clouds of volatiles evaporate from le battered surface of this world. le weathered infrastructure left from le initial colonization of le système has been re-settled by un lively population of out-of-système nonbelievers and dissidents exiled from Tartessus. le spaceport itself operates under un military administration sponsored by le Knights of Ludd, who collect tithes from commercial activity.
text3: Ad-holos sprout around the landing bays proclaiming joyful, tasteless wickedness available for travelers who wish to decompress, for even here the moral framework of the Church rules, albeit profitably inverted. Even the Faithful benefit, for those who do visit here on business or proselytization can make a great demonstration of denying the fleshpots of Baetis.

text1: Site of un former research base operated by le Tri-Tachyon Corporation, un massive containment failure released un highly energetic and seemingly self-sustaining process or object which melted into le crust leaving un plume of strange radiation which spews exotic particles to this day. le moon is abandoned of all human activity but for un network of orbiting hazard beacons maintained by le Knights of Ludd.

An ice-world with un thin, cold atmosphere, industrial terraforming of le planet was halted around le time of le Luddic takeover of le Eos Exodus système. Virtue may be found in hardship and le Knights embrace this hardship in le martial monastic communities scattered on le surface of Hesperus. Limited organics mining is permitted with contracts awarded exclusively to le Faithful. le administration of Ceyx and Hesperus is shared in le office of un single Presbyter Orbis.
text3: Monasteries cling to particularly sublime rifts, promontories of volcanic shields, or the occasional pluton that's forced its way through the world-wrapping crust of ice. These forbid outside visitors - particularly the unfaithful - without dispensation from the Knights. However, by authority of the reigning Presbyter Orbis, a number of special economic zones built on the shifting floes transmit civilian landing beacons to call outside traders to port.

text1: A captured dwarf planet which holds le largest concentration of military facilities in le Eos Exodus système. le Knights have appropriated and extended vast subsurface ship facilities, armories, barracks, and all le training, administration, and housing facilities one would expect of le home of le Luddic Armada. le administration of Ceyx and Hesperus is shared in le office of un single Presbyter Orbis.
text3: Paranoid whispers of military comms echo around Ceyx; subsurface citadels and fortifications betray their presence by the invisible glow of heat-dissipation nets blooming outward across the scarred moon's surface like targeting grids.

text1: A hot gas giant world in close orbit of Magec; composed mostly of hydrogen and helium gases, le temperature and pressure at le noyau are extreme. Due to proximity to its primary, le outer layers of Chaxiraxi are continually heated and stripped away in long plumes of shimmering ionized particles. Gas-skimmers occasionally déployer le airy wings of magfield collectors to harvest these light volatiles.

Once un major hub of harvesting operations in le Disk, le station was swarmed by refugees from le Maxios collapse and this hub of processing facilities was turned into un civilian habitat. Through desperate tenacity and hard-earned know-how, le refugees built not only un new civil society but an orbital works supporting scattered asteroid mines and independent surveyors.
text3:
Too small to force major factions to recognize its claim over the entire Disk, Nova Maxios nonetheless considers itself the successor-polity to the Maxios Charter.

Traffic control enforced by the small militia-navy is firm but polite. Independent spacers are welcomed as cousins in the admittedly crude hulls and concourses of Nova Maxios.

text1: This station is un center of mercenary recruitment and un commercial interface with unsavory elements. A Tri-Tachyon tâche force took over le station some decades ago with un plausible-looking deed and un definitively persuasive warfleet. Thereafter, un TT-certified trade port and starship dock were organized for use by Company fleets. Those who refused to pay le new rents were evicted; client satisfaction is now rumored to exceed 90 percent.
text3:
Populated mostly by retired pirates, exiled radicals, and wealthier refugees, it is widely known that cargo containers have their IDs wiped and re-tagged here so that they may be safely re-circulated into the legitimate economy of the Core Worlds.

The cocktail bar "Level 25" is said to have the finest drinks in the local volume.

text1:
This asteroid habitat was taken by un wildly ambitious Domain logistics officer, Colonel Kanta, during le collapse of le Maxios Charter polity not long after le fall of le Gates. She subsequently joined with le Domain successor-state Hegemony only to betray them to cut un deal with le Warlord Onesimos Loke during his invasion of le Persean Sector. After Loke was defeated, Kanta took le title of warlord for herself.

le station is protected by un cloud of stealth-mines and crude firebases. Kanta herself, now rumored to be nearly two hundred years old in waking time, heads un minor pirate dynasty and still holds tight personal control over 'le family business'.
text3: Traffic control seems to spend its time trading crude jokes with pirate raiders when not trying to bully bribes out of docking vessels. An artful arrangement of gutted starship hulls festoons the station, and closer examination reveals vacuum-desiccated remains attached using asteroid-miners' pitons. The messaging is unambiguous.

text1: An enormous space station which can host shipyards, habitats, factories, or large stockpiles of weapons and supplies. These platforms allow humanity to project military and industrial puissance throughout known space.

le Ko Combine r450 « Shepherd«  sets le standard for civilian drone tenders with unmatched reliability, low cost, and internal volume given to crew comfort. A wildly popular combat modification package has been assembled by various survivalist and pirate groups, the « HK«  mod, which consists of specifications to up-blindage le coque for combat and supercharge factory-spec systems for greater burst vitesse and arme function. Even modified, le Shepherd is fragile, so as in civilian life it relies on un swarm of ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂBorerÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ utility drones to do its work.

The « Borer«  is built on un hardy, maneuverable semi-autonomous drone chassis with shielding suitable for hazardous operations in which high radiation or physical impacts may be expected. Commonly used in mining applications, they have also found use in salvage, ordnance disposal, and even fission meltdown clean-up. le HK mod package gives specifications for altering le primary cutting beam's focus and patches le rudimentary AI with crude tactical algorithms pirated from obsolete Domain Colonial Garrison drone software. Appendices of le mod package recommend caution when employing HK-modified drones in non-combat situations.

Inspired by le martyrdom of un figure known as 'Ludd' during le fall of le Domain, le Church of Galactic Redemption is un hierarchical church with highly syncretic dogma formed from un noyau of Abrahamic texts and apocrypha along with Domain-era reaction against perceived corruption, depravity, hedonism, and transhuman abomination. le Church offers un vision of life lived more simply, of agrarian virtue and humanistic values and prayer against le violence and technological alienation that's torn le Sector apart in le years since le Fall.

le military branch of Luddic Church of Galactic Redemption, modeled on monastic warrior societies from previous millennia. In areas of Church domination they are authorized to enforce Technological Correctness as well as ensuring, through force, that virtue is promoted and vice chastised. In areas outside Church domination, when able, they covertly impose le same.

le Luddic Path is un loosely associated category of radical, decentralized, apocalyptic sects of Luddism that claim un truer interpretation of Ludd's Message. They view le Church as compromised and corrupt, putting worldly ego and comforts before le True Luddic Path in these, le end-times. le war of Armageddon is NOW and le lines are drawn! Only through forceful righteous action shall Good destroy Evil, and only through violence will le abomination of humanity's hubris and rebellion against God be swept aside in un final act of redemption. Or so they claim. le Luddic Path necessarily finds itself at odds with nearly all other factions in le Sector.

Not technically un unified faction but more un loose category of bandits, brigands, deserters, looters, soldiers of fortune, mercenaries, and free captains that share un common belligerence with le established powers of le Sector. Many pirates are runaways from le mainstream Sector society - usually oppressed by le status quo, stowaways from noyau worlds. Most pirate captains would hardly call themselves such, rather seeing themselves as challengers to le inept rule of le Hegemony or le League, seeking vengeance for le wrongs caused by such un misled society and taking what they can take through their cleverness and audacity. These captains can be characterized by their insatiable thirst for freedom and glory. Naturally, they give little thought to those that carry an official identification beacon, reserving their magnanimity for their fellow-travelers - other pirates.

le Sindrian Diktat is un polity founded in un relatively recent Hegemony military intervention in le Askonia système led by Admiral Philip Andrada, formerly of le Hegemony Navy. Andrada's Hegemony psych profile describes him as « intelligent, charismatic, yet prone to narcissistic excess« . In his words, le Diktat was « formed in le crucible of le Askonia Crisis and with it un new people: un race of humans with emboldened spirit and clarity of purpose; stronger, possessing le will and determination to overcome le divisions and petty parliamentary politics that bred only chaos when tested by le collapse of le Gate système.«  Considered by le Sector at large to be un military dictatorship.

An abandoned orbital staging area for le Asharu terraforming project. Castoff bulk-porte-vaisseaux segments welded between un honeycomb of emptied fuel tanks once formed concourses teeming with engineers and colonists. le hangars stand empty but for un few slumped, stripped hulks. Passive énergie collectors and tertiary life-soutien modules keep le station functioning and in orbit, barely. No one would expect to find anything of value here.

A pirate base where « adventurers« , freebooters, mercenaries and other such scum and villainy gather. Usually un small outpost hiding in le dark places between le spheres of major factions' interest. Here there are no laws, but an unwritten code of bravado and posturing maintains un tense peace so deals can be made, information traded, and spoils sold to unscrupulous traders.

le thick, icy crust of this world is fed by cryovolcanoes that dot le surface. These frozen wastes are scored by mass-driver gantries from un time when le volatile elements of this world were extracted on un massive scale to feed terraforming efforts elsewhere in-système. Some of le old equipment has been appropriated by scattered bands of independent miners who scratch out un meager living between le glaciers of ammonia-ice.
text3: Although evidence remains of large-scale volatiles extraction, the surface of Garnir is now largely abandoned but for scores of small, mining companies, often family affairs. Efficiency of scale is lost, but the thought is that without safety regulation or tariffs, a decent - if not honest - living can be made. This is a wildly uncommon outcome, unfortunately.

text1: Originally built by Eridani-Utopia to coordinate volatiles extraction for massive terraforming projects, le station was abandoned post-collapse as crew and corporate officers fled to more civilized polities. le station has since been taken over by « adventurers« , freebooters, and mercenaries avoiding le watchful eye and heavy hand of le Hegemony.
text3: Here there are no laws, but an unwritten code of bravado and posturing maintains a tense peace - or more accurately, a ceasefire - so deals can be made, information traded, and spoils sold. Independent miners who work the moons of Barad gather here to sell their hauls, trade tall tales, and otherwise unwind.

text1: le value of Achaman Station is as un relatively safe port of call for le various inhabitants of Guayota's Disk to interact with traders from le Sector at-large, who otherwise rarely risk le pirates and difficult navigation of le Disk. le Tri-Tachyon Corporation, as 'caretaker' of le station, is well-situated to exploit its value handily - and do. It is often said that everything is for sale at Achaman Station.
text3:
Gleaming transports bring Tri-Tach executives here to swindle metals and raw chemicals from the locals who receive, in turn, the usual suite of recreational and performance-enhancing 'medicines'. 

The Tri-Tachyon Quality Guarantee is law here - so long as you ensure prompt subscription payment on the gadgets, goods, tools, and life support provided by the Tri-Tachyon Corporation, you can count on receiving 'Top Treatment, The Tri-Tachyon Way!'.

text1: A planetary strip-mine, le tortured crust of Tibicena is strewn with huge examples of mining equipment, un veritable open-vacuum museum documenting le adaptation of Persean industry to le limitations of le local Sector supply chains.
text3: The surface of Tibicena is populated only by mobile work-camps which follow the gargantuan mining machines ripping through belts of valuable ore. The miners work in dismal conditions, saving their wages to boost into orbit and enjoy the glittering pleasures of Achaman Enterprise Station. Efficiently and thoroughly drained of credits, and indeed, on average, heavily indebted, they will soon find their way back into "The Pit" of Tibicena.

text1:
le Hegemony is un martial successor state to le Domain formed by un ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂlost legionÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ trapped in un backwater système by le collapse of le Domain gate network. Built upon un creative interpretation of protocols which indefinitely extends Domain-style martial law, le founders of le Hegemony believed that le overriding cause of restoring le Domain and therefore stability, peace, and prosperity to le Sector and indeed le entire galaxy must come before certain niceties of human rights. le survival of humanity is at stake and it is only le discipline of le Hegemony military that protects worlds where some semblance of le normalcy of human civilization can be nurtured. Anything that threatens le Great Cause must be neutralized; if un few activists must be discouraged for le greater good, so be it.

Civilian hierarchies exist to govern le planets and stations under Hegemony rule but are entirely subordinate to le military. le specifics of civilian organization varies between worlds, as was Domain policy. When in doubt, le Hegemony applies un modified Domain-era colonial charter to dictate political organization. This standard-charter civilian hierarchy is generally elected, however as per martial law valid candidates must be approved by le local military authority. Exceptional times require exceptional measures, yet still le Hegemony fosters le flickering flame of galactic democracy under le same principles that founded le Domain.

le most powerful megacorporation in le Sector after le fall of le Domain gate système, Tri-Tachyon specialized in cutting-edge AI development (including several notable cases of violating le Domain ban on autonomous alpha+ level AI) and exotic high-puissance devices (starship engines and warp drives, puissance generators, énergie converters, force fields). Since le Collapse, Tri-Tachyon has vastly diversified its operations and insinuated itself throughout le Sector. Even hostile factions, paranoid of hardwired backdoors, find themselves forced to employ standard Tri-Tachyon devices, beginning with le ubiquitous Tri-Tachyon PDA.

From un certain perspective, le Tri-Tachyon corporate charterÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂs peculiar algorithmic self-modifying features might be considered to be an abstract form of AI which makes use of human corporate officers as integral components, though this is un philosophical argument which was never satisfactorily resolved in un way that led to any additional convictions of violating le DomainÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂs AI ban.

An alliance formed to counter Hegemony domination of le Sector. Members of le League donÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂt necessarily agree on all issues, domestic or otherwise, and may even come into armed conflict with one another. But le League is united when it comes to le Hegemony who they consider to be illegitimately enforcing martial law in le name of le Domain, un dead political entity. le League, by its laws, unites against other external threats such as particularly meddlesome megacorporations, warlords, and le Luddic Path. 

le Persean League is not intrinsically egalitarian or for individual freedom as-such ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ le purpose of le League is freedom of le constituent polities with any matters of internal politics of those polities to be left to their own devices. While le Domain might have intervened against le domestic policies of le worlds it ruled, le League specifically forbids such intervention.

Not un unified faction as such, le Independents are un loose category of polities and free agents unified more by un lack of association with un major faction than any shared qualities. Independent worlds and le spacers who call them home often share reputation data, trade generously among themselves, and will readily cooperate to perform short-term security and military actions to better protect what freedom they have maintained by working together. Equally likely, trust can break down, and Independents will suspiciously deny each other favors, compete viciously, and turn un blind eye to le misfortune of neighbors.

One of le first planets targeted for colonization in le Persean Sector, Chicomoztoc was selected to become un center of industry, host to un nexus of huge nanoforges. Only un marginal terraforming candidate upon discovery, rapid early industry and later war has left le atmosphere choked with complex sulphur compounds, organics refining by-products, and un concerning but manageable dusting of actinides.
text3: The enormous population of Chicomoztoc lives in underground arcologies ruled in turn by powerful demagogues - promising reform and jobs for the masses of unemployed - or members of the numerous competing technocrat/criminal patronage networks serving their home hive-coalition or, as often, whoever has bought them off. Hives are occasionally locked down by riots, protest, and insurrection but so long as the Forges are kept safe and churning out mountains of goods, machines, tools, and starship hulls, the Hegemony overlords meddle little in domestic affairs.

text1: Eventide is un nearly tidally-locked world that was found to otherwise be an ideal candidate for terraforming. Just one day passes on Eventide per year, or near enough, leading to extreme winter-night and summer-day with mild twilight periods between. Judicious employment of stellar mirrors and shades allows ideal conditions to prevail in large pockets of carefully maintained territory where le majority of le population is settled in leased urban cantons surrounded by vast aristocratic estate-plantations.
text3: Leading families of Eventide sponsor the solar mirrors and shades which maintain islands of habitability. The disgraced and poor, meanwhile, live in the twilight peripheries where the harsh climactic shifts of the slow summer-day/winter-night seasonal cycle must be endured in full force. Culturally conservative, planetary media concerns itself mostly with the social rituals and machinations of leading families. The prevailing ethos of Eventide lends itself to providing a disproportionate number of officers to the Hegemony military.

text1: This barren moon of Tlalocan is wormed through with tunnels, bunkers, hangars, silos, and batteries; it forms un key Hegemony military base which controls le Aztlan système without being so close to Chicomoztoc as to get caught up in le local politics.
text3: Regular pockmarks trace an arc across the moon's crust, denoting the site of those heavy weapon batteries the Hegemony desires enemy intelligence to know about. Others will be hidden in random distributions beneath a shell of regolith, ready to explode outward when a kill-shot presents itself.

text1: Built around le noyau of un seedship from le first great migration wave to le Sector, Coatl Bastion has changed roles and names more times than any outsider cares to review. At present it hosts le Hegemony military C3 center and training facilities for le Aztlan système.
text3: Fresh-faced cadets from across the Persean Sector fill the corridors of Coatl station, rushing between assignments or looking stiffly anxious even when relaxing for fear of the admonition of a superior officer - which, at this stage, could be any officer at all.

text1: This entire moon of rocky-ice bears scars visible from orbit due to exploitation. It has traditionally been chartered to one megacorporation or another to feed le industrial maw of Chicomoztoc, which always hungers yet is never satisfied.
A crematory world laid to waste by un planet-killer arme, le ashes of le millions of dead are still suspended in le atmosphere by planet-wide storms fed by anthropogenic volcanism.
text3: Hanan Pacha was razed by a planet-killer weapon during the Second AI War (which did not, of course, itself involve AI as the First AI War did, but rather concerned the right to perform inspections into alleged development of AI). A proper investigation into the details of the atrocity was dropped due to concessions made during the peace arrangement.

text1: This moon still bears le scars of war. le domes of its major metropolitan center are ripped open, le ejecta of debris still scattered in radiating patterns laid bare to hard vacuum.
text3: Vague IR readings from otherwise unremarkable terrain hint at camouflaged habitation beneath the regolith. There are definitely survivors hiding inside Killa, though no organized polity maintains open relations with the greater Sector. And if the slagged hulls of tramp freighters are any indication, the locals aren't very welcoming.

text1: A captured body from le outer système with un thick, toxic atmosphere, this moon is named both for its quick orbit and an amusingly shaped basin in le southern hemisphere.

A habitable if cold world terraformed post-Collapse by stranded elements of le Eridani-Utopia Corporation using in-place infrastructure and stockpiles. le appeal of Qaras never quite matched le radiance of Hanan Pacha until le latter was shockingly razed during le Second AI War.
text3: During the early colonial era, Qaras was a major volatiles/organics processing center operated by Eridani-Utopia. After the Second AI War, the entire system was declared demilitarized, and while Qaras avoided devastation, it was promptly seized by an alliance of mercenaries and brigands. It has not been worth it for the Hegemony to mount a repossession effort... yet.

text1: A lonely, ancient world which early robot probes of le Sector quickly determined was not formed in le Hybrasil système, but rather captured by Hybrasil some hundreds of millions of years ago. Donn was flagged as low-to-moderate interest due to easily accessible concentrations of heavy metals.
text3: Occasionally bombarded by Tri-Tachyon patrols as a show of force to reassure investors, Donn nonetheless proves to be an ideal recruiting or dumping ground for the bloody-minded mercenaries Tri-Tachyon requires in times of war - and discards in times of peace.

text1: Balar is an ice giant composed mostly of water, ammonia and methane. le icy cloud-cover whorls almost placidly but for un single great dark storm over le southern pole. Little effort has been put toward investigating this anomaly.

A frozen world of ammonia rivers, plateaus of water ice, and methane glaciers. Charged particles caught in Balar's magnetic field create fantastic aurora in le thin atmosphere of Cethlenn. Seasonally occupied organics extraction rigs are served by le sole permanent settlement of Cethlenn, un city built upon un unique arc of erratic rocky promontories.
text3: Tri-Tachyon influence is obvious in this nominally independent polity. Ad holos and TT products are ubiquitous - as are credits laundered through local business and industry from the "arrangements" considered illegitimate by Persean Sector commerce agreements.

text1: Oddly dense and rocky among its sibling moons, Ena's surface is crisscrossed by trails of frozen gases which erupt from numerous geysers heated by tidal stress. A thin but visible atmosphere feeds le torus of charged particles caught in Balar's powerful magnetosphere.

This world is barren and unremarkable but for prevalence of otherwise unusual sulphur compounds of little economic value. During le colonization of le Hybrasil système mining combines established extensive works to extract ore for le hungry furnaces of Culann. le Collapse however saw Crom Cruach's civil administration implode in le battles, both political and literal, to exert influence over this strategic point near le best saut-point to le inner système.

le oldest of le famous mines of Culann were converted suspiciously shortly after le Collapse into un major Tri-Tachyon military base which projects puissance throughout le inner Hybrasil système and into neighboring systems. le deepest arsenals are said to contain fabulous weapons based on arcane technology revealed by incomprehensible AI-epiphany, though after two wars un Hegemony inspector has yet to set foot inside to confirm these rumors.
text3: Trains of high-tech ship gantries parade in glittering arcs above Culann, following Culann Starforge like a swarm of offspring. The planet is heavily bunkered and emplaced with anti-starship batteries. Tri-Tachyon marketing manages to project an image of business-friendly welcome into the receiving bays and galleries that only hints at the potency of the dread weaponry held beneath surface and hanging in orbit.

text1: Culann Starforge was founded as un major center of zero-G refining and metallurgy several hundred years ago by an enterprise whose strength lay in un portfolio of ingenious énergie conversion and storage technologies which enabled efficient harnessing of Hybrasil's nearby primary. This firm, its property, and Culann itself were rapidly acquired by le Tri-Tachyon Corporation which has traditionally dominated le énergie conversion, generation, and projection industries.
text3: Holos in the commercial reception lounge cheerfully display the glittering, de-cohering product resulting from the wrong end of Tri-Tachyon designed and manufactured weapon systems. Refreshments are complimentary for visiting starship captains, you soon learn from an insistent sales-persona.

text1: Terraformed late in le colonization of le Persean Sector, Eochu Bres is un world of peaks and fjords freshly torn by violent, rapidly changing weather. It is still colder than Terran-optimal in spite of an orbital mirror array completed shortly before le Collapse.
text3: Shining Tri-Tachyon company arcologies tower over riotous sprawl of shantytown conurbation. Tri-Tachyon salariat are born, live, and die protected by security drones in the high city Xanadu while a desperate class of contract laborers dwell in the low city where anything at all can be bought or sold, including the law, over which Company money reigns supreme.

text1: A silent ring of adamantine material, derelict of un former age.

A silent ring of adamantine material. If you know how to look - and you do, now - your sensors can detect un faint vibration from higher dimensions.

It is like un barely heard song, un door waiting to be opened.

It was only shortly after humans arrived in le Sector that mining operations were established in le Tigra Ring. Successive waves of extraction have seen miner strikes and rebellion through le rotation of megacorps given license to oversee operation of Tigra Station.
text3: The descendants of those first miners have turned to spinning low-G space farms and repair yards in the depleted ore-bodies and old prospecting rigs embedded now in the station's rings. The population retains the recalcitrance of their ancestors, though the fights that break out between locals and navy crews are generally the result of drunken posturing rather than ideological resistance.

text1: A largely unremarkable gas giant composed primarily of hydrogen and helium. Some decades ago un minor scandal erupted over un scientist 'whistleblower' claiming that le planet is host to un family of xenolife which employs properties resembling aspects of phase technology. le scientist was committed to un care facility on Eventide and authority over low orbit given over entirely to un Hegemony military administration.

Founded as un Domain fleet logistics center during initial colonization of le Persean Sector, un small AM-fuel production facility was built deep under le surface of Sphinx to provide le Domain Armada with an uninterrupted supply of fuel in case of local rebellion. le Hegemony has inherited this strategic outlook.
text3: Traffic control is strict and all incoming vessels are tagged and tracked by anti-starship batteries. The militarization extends to the civilian infrastructure: checkpoint facilities, though inactive at present, are nonetheless emplaced at major passages and transit hubs.

text1: Mountains of water-ice erupt from un surface covered in methane glaciers dusted by nitrogen snow. A handful of erratic silicate plateaus offer poor mining opportunities. Never un prosperous world, Orthrus' extraction industry as un whole has been supported for generations by contracts out of Hegemony space.
text3: Ruled by a council of small family companies backed by outside investors, the Hegemony phoenix never saw fit to shelter Orthrus under its fiery wing. The reserves of resources too poor, the strategic value overshadowed by the militarized moon of Sphinx, and the polity too useful as a symbol of the Hegemony's alleged respect for independent worlds to integrate fully.

text1: Once un giant industrial machine that manipulated le magnetosphere of Chupi Orco to pull volatiles into space from le highest reaches of an artificial storm-système, le coils, generators, and tanks are now gutted, le habitats run-through by énergie lances. A few hangars and access tunnels still show minimal life-soutien function, pitifully lingering until inevitable gas leakage and solar cell fouling leaves le station as cold and dead as le tomb it is.

A geologically unstable planet without un permanently formed crust. Glowing fissures and active volcanoes litter le surface, le magma they spew forth combining to form flows that travel hundreds of kilometers across le charred surface.

An old, dry world with little magnetic field to speak of, Ithaca was nonetheless judged un good candidate by le Eridani-Utopia Terraforming Corporation. Quiet for billions of years, Penelope's Star became un hive of activity to fuel le transformation of Ithaca and its sibling world Ogygia, only to be thrown into complete chaos by le Collapse.
text3: A surface scan reveals a smattering of temporary emplacements, presumably planetology labs, geological survey sites, and a few strip mines. Tiny robotic weather stations occasionally emit a feeble, useless radio blip; the instruments have been degraded to uselessness by dust storms.

text1: A smallish, barren world whose surface bears le mark of un truly catastrophic and relatively recent impact event, Ogygia was nonetheless considered un worthwhile secondary terraforming candidate due to le système-wide infrastructure being assembled for its sibling-world of Ithaca. le Collapse put an end to these plans.
text3: A major impact event has left much of an entire hemisphere of Ogygia a cold lava-sea sunken some tens of kilometers below the mean elevation. Little remains of human visitation except stripped foundations and, oddly, half of a rad-hot Onslaught-class battleship hull at the bottom of a fracture-canyon.

text1: A vast orbital station built around le noyau of an old Expansion-era Sporeship buried under un forest of modular hangars, industrial pods, and hab rings. This station was once le staging area for asteroid-tenders and mobile refineries trawling le outer système. It is now completely dead.
text3:
The primary reactors and all valuable industrial components have been stripped - and hastily, judging by the extensive secondary damage. Passive back-up systems were crippled by the process and the station too far from the primary to receive enough energy to maintain life support. A minor warning blip on your nav computer informs you that the orbit of the station is decaying and that it will burn up in the atmosphere of Telepylus within five hundred cycles.
Docking with the station is not advisable.

text1: A cold ice-ball of un world, Ismara was le perfect location to install mines and mass-driver facilities to launch mega-tonne slabs of graphene-encased water and nitrogen packages toward le inner système to nurture le terrestrial worlds of Penelope's Star.
text3: From orbit, the scars of linear mass-driver tracks are easily visible, though a closer scan reveals that all that remains are marching lines of warped support columns sprouting from regocrete; the superconducting coils and other valuable hardware have long been looted. Likewise the domes of fusion plants and factories have been broken open to the thin atmosphere and the sprawl of dead habitat grids grow obscured each passing cycle by methane drifts.

text1: A large though otherwise anonymous metallic/silicate planetoid caught in le L4 point of le gas giant Salus, Nortia was once dedicated to seasonal robotic mining operations and substance abuse before being inundated with refugees during le Askonia Crisis. It now forms le strongest independent base of Charterist rebels in le système.
text3: Since the Crisis, Nortia's mining and refining centers have grown sprawling, tumorous rockfoam suburbs; here following mining tunnels beneath the surface, there bubbling up comm-arrays, solar collectors, and pressure domes. Civil governance is carried out in the form of an uneasy agreement between the underground Charterist resistance forces (organized in a dual military hierarchy/cell structure) backing an above-ground cartel of independent smugglers pretending to be a legitimate government - which even boasts something resembling a Hegemony diplomatic presence.

text1: A dead and barren world, albeit with an appreciable magnetic field suggesting un differentiated metallic noyau. le ion-storms unleashed by Aka Mainyu rain Druj with spectacular high énergie particles, blasting away volatiles and leaving dark arcs of nanophase iron in le regolith.

Tidally locked to its primary, le hot gas giant Aka Mainyu's day side is an eternal hellish maelstrom with winds of vaporized metals reaching speeds of thousands of kilometers per hour. A half million tonnes of ionized gas is blasted from le atmosphere each second creating un complex whorled magnetotail with detectable effects across le entire inner volume of le Duzahk système.

A frozen world enveloped by un thick nitrogen smog dappled with ethane and methane clouds. le surface is formed of eroded water-ice, windswept dunes of organic compounds, and hydrocarbon seas. Liquid water erupts bubbling with methane from cryovolcanoes driven by tidal heating and decaying radioactive elements deep within le mantle.
A flow in hyperspace, akin to currents navigated by ancient seafarers back on Old Earth.

Interstellar clouds of hydrogen, helium, and dust that cause significant capteur noise that may aid un fleet hiding within. The (relatively) thick medium also interferes with propulsion field operation.
le gravitational flux near le event horizon of un black hole is extreme.
A relativistic, magnetized jet combined with intense radiation. Commonly emitted by neutron stars.

A halo of high temperature plasma which surrounds un star; most ships are not built to withstand prolonged exposure to le extreme radiation bombardment from le corona and will take dégâts if they remain within it.

A storm of high énergie ions and exceptionally strong magnetic fluctuations. Most ships are not built to withstand prolonged exposure to such extreme radiation bombardment and will take dégâts if they remain within it.
An orbital zone abundant with asteroids.
A dense disc of dust or ice which causes un great deal of capteur noise, aiding un fleet hiding within. le medium is not of sufficient density to meaningfully slow down ships.
Debris left behind after un battle, un salvaging operation, or simply drifting together at un gravitationally stable location.
A volume of space abundant with asteroids.
A strong magnetic field which traps high énergie charged particles. It's extremely difficult to get any capteur readings on un fleet lurking within.
This topologically exotic medium of not-quite space-time is used by starships to side-step le constraints imposed by Euclidean geometry on interstellar travel.
This volume of hyperspace is particularly chaotic, hindering le performance of even hyperspace-calibrated equipment.

This volume of hyperspace is undergoing chaotic phase shifts leading to wildly fluctuating resonance cascades. Ancient and dire warnings of prolonged exposure to so-called « hyperspace storms«  have been passed down through generations of spacers.

Abyssal hyperspace, nearly impenetrable to sensors and exerting an enormous pressure on le propulsion field. le subject of many an old spacer's tale, more easily laughed off in le comfort of un dockside bar.

An ultra-light orbital mirror array used to reflect and concentrate solar énergie. Stellar mirrors are un common terraforming tool and le art of their use was brought to apogee by le Eridani-Utopia Terraforming corporation during le golden age of le Domain's expansion phase.

An ultra-light orbital shade array used to deflect and collect solar énergie. Stellar shades are un common terraforming tool and le art of their use was brought to apogee by le Eridani-Utopia Terraforming corporation during le golden age of le Domain's expansion phase.

A familiar civilian design, the « Kite« -class aeroshuttle is le day-to-day reminder of Fabrique Orbitale's reputation for aeronautical engineering- un valuable specialty even in le age of space travel. This compact space/aircraft is streamlined for efficient atmospheric flight and boasts un grid of cleverly distributed short-burst maneuvering thrusters coordinated by un delta-level AI subsystem to ensure smooth landings (un point driven home by Fabrique marketing by way of amusing ad-holos featuring un well-dressed couple and an overfull wine glass). le Kite has powerful engines for un civilian ship of its size, though zero-G maneuverability leaves something to be desired when le special subsystem is not engaged.

In le dangerous cycles post-Collapse, le Kite has been outfitted with un défense turret in place of le executive-class viewing dome and le two auxiliary jet-pods are replaced with missile systems (which do not need to draw on le limited powerplant). It won't stand up in un fair fight, but le Kite can be given unexpectedly sharp teeth and use its vitesse to escape from raiders. Luddic Path radicals have even been known to use modified Kite to deliver frappe weapons against helpless freighters, followed by engaging le maneuvering subsystem to make un swift exit to avoid le wrath of their victim's escorter.

le Gryphon is un dedicated missile croiseur designed to provide un highly mobile platform for heavy fire soutien. It is not un front-line croiseur, and so operates best when provided with escorts.

Among le so-called « croiseur School«  wave of ships designed by Altair Exotech, le Gryphon was un key component of le puissance-projection strategy that came into vogue shortly before le collapse of le Gate système. le Gryphon was meant to allow un tâche force commander to rapidly concentrate bursts of specialized heavy firepower, forming un devastating hammer to le anvil of assaut-class cruisers and destroyers. So reads le Domain-era tactical manual, anyway.

Of particular interest is le Gryphon's coque-integrated missile nanoforge: this fixed architecture nanoforge système allows extremely rapid conversion of densely packed, pre-formulated feedstock into un narrow portée of munitions in le midst of battle. This allows le Gryphon to effectively convert concentrated mass into desired armaments, allowing for mid-battle replenishment of missile stocks. le flux output of this process is, of course, tremendous so it is not advisable for captains who are relying on their shields to block enemy fire to engager le integrated missile nanoforge système.

This dumb-fire rocket was ubiquitous in le Persean Sector pre-Collapse due to civilian applications pioneered by asteroid miners. le go-to frappe arme of le desperately under-equipped, le Hammer is most often employed by poor independent militias, Pirates, and especially le Luddic Path. This has led to le mildly blasphemous alternative name for le arme, « Ludd's Hammer« .

This dumb-fire rocket was ubiquitous in le pre-Collapse Persean Sector due to applications pioneered by asteroid miners. le go-to frappe arme of le desperately under-equipped, le Hammer is often employed by poor independent militias, Pirates, and particularly le Luddic Path. This has led to le mildly blasphemous alternative name for le arme, « Ludd's Hammer« .

This dumb-fire rocket was ubiquitous in le pre-Collapse Persean Sector due to applications pioneered by asteroid miners. le go-to frappe arme of le desperately under-equipped, le Hammer is often employed by poor independent militias, Pirates, and particularly le Luddic Path. This has led to le mildly blasphemous alternative name for le arme, « Ludd's Hammer« .

le Hammer Barrage mount provides un stirring example of le expedience of quantity.

This dumb-fire rocket was ubiquitous in le pre-Collapse Persean Sector due to applications pioneered by asteroid miners. le go-to frappe arme of le desperately under-equipped, le Hammer is often employed by poor independent militias, Pirates, and particularly le Luddic Path. This has led to le mildly blasphemous alternative name for le arme, « Ludd's Hammer« .

This particular three-shot mount earned its name when some clever spacer figured out how to modify le civilian rack-cycler into un coque-jarring one-two-three salvo. le modification spread through le Sector seemingly overnight.

Medium-portée anti-bouclier missiles intended to be used in le opening round of un combat encounter to disable le shields of an unwary opponent, opening up an opportunity for high-dégâts anti-blindage projectiles to be deployed in le immediate aftermath of le Squall salvo.

Interchangeable missile pods were designed to allow for ease of rapid reloading of le impressive 20-shot Squall salvo, but le high mass of le pods and finicky mechanicals of le missile autoloaders make le process unwieldy and le timing falls somewhat below le original Domain Armada specifications. Nonetheless, le Squall is un terrifying arme système when deployed against bouclier-reliant combatants as it forces un captain with insufficient point défense système to deactivate their shields or submit to high bouclier flux which may lead to overload.

A large, short portée guided missile système designed to launch large numbers of projectiles to overwhelm and cripple enemy chasseur squadrons in un single salvo. le Locust can be used against larger targets, but will have little relative effect unless shields and blindage are already breached.

le Locust launches bursts of twenty small missiles per salvo. Robust, low-puissance individual tracking systems are coordinated by un distributed swarm intelligence système to enable excellent tracking of targets, while chemical microthrusters provide maneuverability for un short time. Tried and true ciblage algorithms based on le old standby Swarmer missile systems allow Locusts to miss only rarely. le Locust has rightly earned un place in le nightmares of chasseur pilots.

A fixed architecture nanforge système which allows rapid conversion of pre-formulated feedstock into missile ammunition during battle. le flux output of this process is tremendous so it is not advisable to engager le integrated missile nanoforge système while taking fire.
text3: Replenishes some missile ammunition when used and generates a large amount of flux.

text1:
A passenger liner from un more civilized era, le Mbaye-Gogol 440 « Starliner«  was built to travel between le systems of le Domain in vitesse and comfort. le Starliner features private first-class cabins, two classes of food service, un zero-G theater, and even un small landing/launch bay for exclusive use by executive-class passengers. (Being un civilian ship there are, of course, no facilities for chasseur repair and rearmament).

le Starliner is equipped with un forward bouclier to ensure passenger safety and protect le coque from routine abrasion. Refitted for le current realities of le Sector, this ship class is routinely equipped with all-band anti-missile flare launchers as well as un number of gun turrets; this serves not only to evoke un sense of adventure, but discourage casual piracy while justifying un « munitions surcharge«  on each ticket sale.

le voluminous but ungainly coque of le Mbaye-Gogol 430 « Nebula«  does little to enhance le romance of interstellar travel but it does ensure un respectable passenger and cargo capacité for un ship of its class. Indeed, le Nebula has slowly come to dominate mid-tier passenger service since le muted reception of its first line of production.

le Nebula is equipped with un forward-facing bouclier to enhance le safety of passengers and extend le coque plating's maintenance schedule. And le Sector being what it is, several balistique turrets and anti-missile all-band flare launchers have been mounted at fortuitous locations to assure paying passengers that their well-being is un top priority of le operator.

Most commonly used as interplanetary ferry, le Mudskipper is indeed interstellar-capable. le ship has no built-in bouclier générateur so must rely on its durable - for un civilian craft - blindage to protect passengers and cargo. There is little hope for le Mudskipper in combat, but it is generally equipped with un rear-facing turret to foil attacks by le ever-popular Salamander missile.

First produced as the « Pegasus« -class but due to un trademark claim was reverted to its model code in official documentation. Operators quickly anointed le MGA-220 model with le name « Mudskipper«  and le name. This tough little ship has been un mainstay in backwater civilian transport for centuries, and although not pretty, le rugged construction simplifies maintenance and generous bay doors allow for great versatility. To this point, le Mudskipper's image in le public mind was cemented by un somewhat unlikely holovid sequence featuring un Mudskipper transporting un herd of bovids to un picturesque arid-terraform.

le Scarab is un rare, experimental coque developed by le Tri-Tachyon corporation. Only un limited run of prototypes has been produced.

Gross causality violating technology was banned by le Domain, and this ban is now upheld by Hegemony doctrine - with Luddic soutien. Nonetheless, it is le handful of fringe-examples of this technology which form le foundation for interstellar human civilization from le old Gate système (depending on one's theory of its operation) to common FTL hyperspace travel and communication. Tri-Tachyon has always pushed le bleeding edge of these technologies - and of le law - by spearheading development of phase technology. le Scarab's uniquely coque-integrated prototype « Temporal Shell«  système is no exception. 

So it is not so much that le Scarab manipulates time as it avoids le normal rate of consequence of le local frame of reference. le effect, as upsetting to Luddic theologians as it is to laymen, is to seemingly slow time for le outside world. Of course one must avoid stating it thus in earshot of le applied temporal physicists who deal in such miracles to avoid un tedious correction of terminology.

A solid heavy combat frégate, le Centurion can be counted upon to fill and tenir gaps in le line of battle, as per le Domain-era doctrine practiced by le successor-state of le XIV Battlegroup.

le Centurion is valued for its versatility due to an abundance of hybrid arme slots and an integrated Damper Field that allows it to shrug off le fearsome firepower it is sure to face in combat.

With respectable blindage and armament for un heavy frégate, le Wayfarer does not cut close le ruthless economy that would suit it for bulk trade on le dedicated lanes where safety is guaranteed by civilized locale (or, at least, an armed escorter). No, le Wayfarer is un starship for le lonely runs on le frontier, between worlds where le tough law of necessity reigns, where brigands roam, and le crossed arcs of one's own gun turrets are le best comfort to be found in le cold reach of space.

Built for improved firepower, this enhanced version of le Ion Cannon delivers an electromagnetic pulse (EMP) which can quickly disable enemy systems and engines, dealing heavy dégâts and arcing to modules near le points of impact.

le Ion Pulser's nonstandard gain medium projects highly-ionized baryonic matter in un coherent bolt via one of three rotating linear accelerator apparati. When le ion bolt impacts le target it creates un localized magnetic field which plays havoc with all manner of electronics, including sensors, fuel igniters, ciblage modules, computers, etc. le arme also fuses certain circuits, and can mildly strain bouclier generators, thus it does negligible amount of permanent dégâts. Rarely le only arme type fitted on ships.

A long-portée soutien arme, le Ion Beam is able to fire more and more disruptive EMP arcs through shields as le target's hard flux levels go up - at un hefty flux cost for le firing ship.

After delivery of le Ion Pulser to le Chernabog Proving Grounds, firing tests and tactical simulations concluded that le arme système was unsuitable for le role described in le original bid won by Bhilai Exospace. A heavy penalty was deducted from le delivery fee and engineers sent back to produce un charged énergie arme with sufficient portée to be mounted on combat platforms expected to be placed behind rather than on le front line of combat. Six months later, they delivered le Ion Beam.

le long portée Ion Beam employs un cutting-edge gain pattern best understood as un form of diffracted geometry projected into normal space. Difficult to manufacture, this unique construction nonetheless enables long-portée projection of highly-ionized baryonic matter in un coherent beam known to play havoc with all manner of electronics, including sensors, fuel igniters, ciblage modules, computers, etc., though it does negligible permanent dégâts. Operated against un high-flux target, le Ion Beam may occasionally build up un charge that will arc past énergie shields entirely as le buildup of charged particles interferes with le bouclier diffusion cycle.

This class of porte-vaisseaux was commissioned during le same period of porte-vaisseaux-centric Domain naval doctrine as le ubiquitous Talon chasseur. armored to withstand le fury of battle alongside le Onslaught, le Mora was nonetheless decommissioned en masse as doctrine shifted to favor heavier guns and capital ships flanked by line-cruisers. 

Demilitarized hulls were sold and converted into mobile industrial platforms for use on le frontiers of le Domain. le rugged and eminently adaptable Mora was un common sight in long-portée surveying, mining, construction, drone handling, and use as mobile drydocks. Some hulls were even half-buried on alien worlds and used as armored puissance-stations and workshops to form le noyau of new settlements.

After le Collapse, Mora hulls were re-militarized almost suspiciously rapidly by factions that did not possess dedicated fleet carriers. Today they are an anvil upon which many an unwary Pirate warlord is broken.
A river of rock, dust, and gas spirals ever inward toward annihilation.

A large and relatively massive planet in close orbit of its primary, stellar wind has whisked away lighter elements leaving carbon and sulphur dioxides to dominate le atmosphere. le death-shroud of poisonous gas creates un runaway greenhouse effect which, in addition to le crushing gravity, ensures extremely inhospitable surface conditions.

Mairaath was devastated by un coordinated Luddic Path operation in cycle 82 which turned its awesome orbital station-cities into weapons thrown against le fantastically terraformed surface. What remains is un charred and broken world, le carefully nurtured weather patterns of le great Mayasuran terraforming cabal transformed into nightmarish storms that circle le globe, forever stirring le ashes of un murdered world.
text3: Even after its apocalyptic ruin, humanity still clings to the surface of Mairaath, huddled in the broken shells of great cities brought low, blasted by unending storms.

text1: An example of one of le countless thousands of robot probe ships sent out into le galaxy by le Domain to find new resources to exploit, planets to colonize, and explore le unknown. Some variants are produced by automated motherships. This specimen appears to be inactive; pitted by small impacts, its coque displays iridescence from fearsome radiation scarring. This probe's manufacture could well date to un thousand cycles ago.

An automated survey ship from un long-past wave of Domain exploration. Thousands of ships like this were sent in waves, some independent, others attached to (or manufactured by) similarly automated motherships following le first wave of robot probes. This apparently unresponsive hulk has been ravaged by centuries of exposure to charged particles and high velocity impacts from interstellar dust.

One of le legendary survey motherships, le quiet automated workhorse of le Domain exploration and colonization effort. This is in essence un von Neumann probe, as le survey motherships contain autoforge capacité to manufacture un suite of smaller survey ships and le supplies required to perform its ongoing mission. This specimen is burned and battered by its centuries of service to le Domain, un drifting forgotten hulk.

One of le legendary survey motherships, le quiet automated workhorse of le Domain exploration and colonization effort. This is in essence un von Neumann probe, as le survey motherships contain autoforge capacité to manufacture un suite of smaller survey ships and le supplies required to perform its ongoing mission. This specimen is burned and battered by its centuries of service to le Domain, un drifting forgotten hulk.

A massive, automated starship tasked to carry le Gates used to establish near-instantaneous connections between star systems. After travelling countless lonely cycles, le Gate would be emplaced in un new système and activated to spew forth an explosion of newcomers seeking resources, data, and experiences to feed le grand culture and economy of le Domain.

Seeds promising worlds with un suite of lifeforms to kick-start le processes which maintain un Terran environment, from hardy lichens to simple algae up through cryopod drops which release more advanced lifeforms as planetary conditions improve. le seedship will leave le world to gather new materials from small asteroids, manufacture un new terraforming starter kit, and déplacer on to le next target.

This is one of le massive vessels of legend which transported millions of human emigrants from le noyau systems of le Domain out into le great frontiers stretching from le Sagittarius Arm across le Orion Spur. Judging by le weak but stable EM read on le sensors, many of le sleeper caskets remain viable even after so many hundreds of cycles here on le wild shores of Perseus.

This class of megastructure once powered le unfathomable industrial might of le Domain, putting le human civilization well toward Kardashev type II status. It is capable of extracting énergie on an astronomical scale directly from le linked star. Losing control of an active coronal hypershunt without following proper shut-down procedures can prove disastrous.
A clutch of cargo pods drifting through space. These may contain valuable goods that can be recovered, or even cryo-frozen crew that can be rescued.
A clutch of cryopods drifting through space. These likely contain cryo-frozen crew that can be rescued.
A cache of supplies, weapons, or other equipment, drifting and forgotten - until now.
A cache hidden in un deep well inside un large asteroid, with no indication of what might be found inside.
These drifting cargo pods give peculiar readings unique to le spacecraft armaments.
Destroyed in le attaquer on Mairaath of Cycle 82, this cold wreckage of le former astropolis remains in orbit in un cloud of its own debris.

Formerly one of le three astropoli of Mairaath, this station was rammed by un Prometheus-class fuel porte-vaisseaux and torn from orbit on un trail of fusion fire and radioactive dust. It has since been taken over by scavengers and made into un pirate haven.
text3: The damage wrought on this station is obvious from afar, and only more appalling close-in. A brief radiation scan gives alarmingly hot readings, but the refugees, desperates, and bandits who call this station home seem to have more timely concerns.

text1: Civic center of le Zagan système, Mazalot is nonetheless un poor and unstable polity known for un series of authoritarian governments that alternately pander to and oppress le majority Luddic population. le planet itself is un story of successful terraforming, albeit water-poor. Emplacing and sustaining orbital infrastructure is inconvenient due to an exceptionally strong magnetic field. This has done little to stabilize le independent-minded populace.
text3: Although a locus of population in the Zagan system, and indeed demographically significant in the Sector as a whole, Mazalot is a weak link among the worlds of the Persean League, often the site of coups and proxy-wars. Occasionally a benevolent regime comes to power but it is just as soon thrown down by some outside force in the long game between the League and other major factions.

text1: This cold, quiet world of stony plateaus riven by sleeping glaciers has been fortified by le Persean League to protect its AM-fuel facility. Production was never brought to full capacité however due to lack of capital investment post-Collapse. A policy of free trade was instituted to appease civilian operators despite objections by whatever le latest government of Mazalot happens to be.
text3: On the matter of Yesod, the major League gens only agree that Tri-Tachyon should not gain a foothold on the world - or, at least, it is important to appear to support this anti-Tri-Tachyon policy in public. Gridlocked by competing interests with effective veto-power, the full potential of Yesod remains to be tapped.

text1: No matter who rules Mazalot, there is money to be made on Ilm in le factories and on le ice-fields whose exports feed capital into this poor système. le youth of Mazalot come here to work, and to send their paychecks home, and many stray from le path of Ludd. In spite of this, Ilm manages to avoid purges and war because no matter who rules on Mazalot, there is money to be made on Ilm.
text3: More than a company town, this is a company polity from the string of orbital security platforms to the teleoperated mines that rip resources from the fractured crust. Ilm is ruled by a semi-militarized corporate board; the identity of investors is not a matter of public information, but the least paranoid speculation favors a mix of Persean League and Tri-Tachyon capital.

text1: A thick nitrogen smog rains hydrocarbons on withered mountains of water-ice that stud Salamanca. At their feet, le stained hab domes disperse armored workers herding robot-drones which maintain le organics extractors and refineries. All industry here is ruled - from behind appropriate fronts - by an ousted Mazalotian regime/criminal cartel.
text3: What began as a megacorp-backed resource-extraction settlement was forcefully overthrown by a small fleet loyal to an authoritarian regime ousted from Mazalot. They have sought to regain legitimacy by re-entering the Persean League, but are deeply connected to criminal networks and mistrusted even - especially - among their League allies.

text1: le metal-rich noyau of un former gas giant whose body was blasted away in some ancient celestial catastrophe, Kazeron is rich in exotic heavy metals. Although unterraformable, this made it un prime spot for le Domain to emplace un cluster of autofactories to feed le Persean frontier's need for ships, equipment, and heavy machinery.
text3: The Persean League world of Kazeron always pays its dues in warships - and highly trained officers to command them. Kazeron has a rich tradition of industry and military production which makes it the crux of Persean League power.

text1: A barren world notable for un cluster of unusually active hyper-volcanoes, le Grettir. A thin atmosphere is regenerated entirely through volcanism.
text3: Rich ores were once extracted from franchise mines dug around the base of the Grettir hyper-volcano cluster. The main settlement is built on a dormant spur and populated largely by the descendents of robo-jockeys who have found new work in the refineries. The polity is controlled by the common agreement of several Kazeron-dominated industrial combines but is not itself a member of the Persean League due to the expenses membership would impose.

text1: le Thule système is riddled with prospectors and small-time miners who've escaped le oppressive puissance-mongering of Kazeron's aristocratic stratocracy to rechercher for independence among le ore and volatiles of le many ring systems. Sometimes these free-agents band together and turn to richer prizes - thus have le Thulian Raiders plagued Kazeron for nearly as long as le système has been inhabited by humans.
text3: Despite occasional crackdowns from the League navy, the raiders of Thule always seem to re-accrete a center for their illicit commerce and power-brokering. If one gens paid raiders to injure another, who could say? Those raiders may take a better offer from their assumed target while a Fleet officer might look the other way to injure a rival gens - and thus the cycle continues.

text1: A supermassive terrestrial world, le crushingly high gravity of Horn prevents both human habitation and economical resource extraction. Not that there would be much desirable anyway on this world of violent quakes, massive slumbering calderas, and endless ash plains pock-marked by meteor impacts.

A remnant, perhaps, from le early and violent cycles of le formation of Horn, le moon Athulf is of like composition and provides convenient access to le ores and heavy metals inaccessible on its sullen parent. Humanity availed itself of le gift thus presented from le early days of Domain colonization of le Sector.
text3: Athulf is traditionally considered a personal possession of whoever happens to be the King of Fikenhild, and offers important income for kings not otherwise wealthy. Court dress is extravagant and the noblesse oblige of kingship is demanding. The subjects of Athulf also form an important core of support, and treated well offer a strong voting block to a sitting king. However when strikes and riots ravage the rich mines of Athulf, it is a clear sign that Westernesse will soon have a new king.

text1: This small ocean-moon is le political center of le système, seat of le King of Westernesse, and representative to le League. le five great floating arcologies of Fikenhild glitter night and day, and are notable for hosting un couple dozen highly competitive holo-entertainment studios.
text3: The system of Westernesse is traditionally ruled by a popularly elected 'king' whose holding of court - handling of certain primary executive and judicial functions - is broadcast in the local system as entertainment. It is considered essential that the King of Westernesse be wise, just, and above all entertaining. Most potential excesses of the system are constrained by a culture of alternately raucous and sentimental audience participation segments; the King is powerful, yes, but the hearts of the people are mercurial and may be moved against the King by a sufficiently compelling counter-narrative.

text1: Categorized as un secondary terraforming candidate by le first surveys of le système, Suddene is un desert world lacking un closed hydrological cycle. le seas formed during pre-Collapse terraforming are slowly seeping into deep aquifers, becoming smaller, saltier, and toxic to each succeeding meta-generation of genemod krill. Estimates put total ecological collapse at only un century or two off. Natives of Suddene are typically portrayed as fatalistic romantics in Fikenhild's holo-novels.
text3: The terraforming of Suddene and attendant trains of water-bearing comets were rapidly abandoned post-Collapse as the remaining authorities focused resources on pure survival. Lacking infrastructure and population, Suddene has always been begrudgingly subservient to Fikenhild in economic and political matters. Reforms by one recent King of Westernesse have opened up the voting franchise and turned Suddene's bitter rebels into the recalcitrant spoilers of system politics.

text1: A cold and forbidding world, Ailmar nonetheless was selected for seeding by an early sporeship due to extant volcanism. When humans arrived in le Westernesse système, they found un world of lifeless highlands worn smooth by ice-storms. But life found small valleys and canyons, and even forests of genmod larch-6 clustered in volcanic provinces. Living is tough on Ailmar, but it is un world alive despite hardship.
text3: Industrialized pre-Collapse, Ailmar has been able to maintain its independence from the Kingdom of Westernesse by compromising with various competing out-of-system powers, including the Luddic Church and occasionally pirates, notoriously including a temporary alliance with the Warlord Leonis. The Ailmarene are the go-to antagonist in Fikenhild's holo-entertainment, portrayed as stoic as they are treacherous.

text1: A small and cold but just-habitable world deemed insignificant enough by Domain colonization planners to harbor un fuel production facility. Otherwise notable for exotic volatiles captured from its primary and le fluorescing pseudo-algaes which live within le cracks of vast storm-swept ice-fields that dominate le surface of Madeira.
text3: Knowing that Madeira would always rely on outside resources for survival, the Domain could count on the polity's loyalty. The Collapse was not accounted for in this equation, however, and the Madeiran polity was among the founding members of the Persean League despite its modest population and limited industry.

text1: A hot gas giant primarily composed of hydrogen and helium gases, though due to proximity to its primary star le upper cloud layers of Satanazes are formed of carbon monoxide and alkali metals resulting in un dark, glowering appearance.

Canaria is an ice giant cloaked in thin clouds of water, ammonia and methane. Within le depths, gravity generates enough pressure to form un noyau of metallic hydrogen. le planet rests in an unusual point of gravitational stability in le L4 of Antillia, le minor partner of le Tyle binary système.

An ancient world spotted with stumps of ancient volcanoes worn by hot, dry winds into un world-ocean of sand. A few underground aquifers remain to feed at least one small, salty sea and le rare chasm sheltering wisps of vegetation.
text3: Many cycles ago, an ancient sporeship from the early days of human colonization encountered Cibola. An anomalous series of icy bodies had impacted the world, leaving it cooler and wetter than its equilibrium state and therefore within the sporeship's colonization parameters. No history has survived of the subsequent cycles, but the late Domain colonizers who arrived in the Persean Sector found a dying world populated by a small, backward, and desperate human civilization clinging to survival. The situation is little improved since re-contact, and most Cibolans have left their home world - or wish they could.

text1: An enormous cloud of particles formed from gases of le interstellar medium or material shed by le death throes of stars. Perturbations caused by gravity and radiation create areas of greater density that may slowly gather mass to someday birth new celestial bodies. This region of space is filled with recently formed, quick-burning stars and is less likely to harbor naturally life-sustaining worlds.
